reasoning: While text recall is nearly perfect (0.995), the LLM fidelity signals identify two significant structural and visual defects: a break in heading hierarchy (skipping H2) which violates accessibility standards, and the total loss of a substantive infographic on Page 2. Given that false negatives are 3x more costly and the composite fidelity score (0.8443) sits below the high-confidence threshold of 0.85, these major findings require human intervention to ensure accessibility and informational completeness.

concerns.analysis: Section 2 contains a break in heading hierarchy. The main title of the section ('Preparing for the Census – We Have Work To Do!') acts as a level 1 heading, but the subsequent subsection titles (e.g., 'Boundary Annexation Survey (BAS)', 'Local Update of Census Addresses (LUCA)') are assigned level 3 headings. This skips level 2 entirely, which is a structural defect according to accessibility standards.
